seoTitle: Sync Slicers in Power BI Across Multiple Pages
metaDescription: Learn how to sync slicers across pages in Power BI so your filters stay consistent no matter which report page a viewer is on.
intro: Keeping filters consistent as viewers move between report pages makes for a much smoother experience. This guide covers how to set up and sync slicers in Power BI across pages, ensuring your data selections carry through your entire report.

steps.heading: Create slicers in Power BI
steps.body: Start by adding the slicer visuals you want to use for filtering, placing them on the pages where they're needed.

steps.heading: Set up slicer sync
steps.body: Use Power BI's sync slicers feature to link a slicer across multiple pages so the same selection applies everywhere it's synced.

steps.heading: Follow best practices for syncing
steps.body: Decide deliberately which slicers should sync and which shouldn't, since not every filter needs to apply across your entire report.

steps.heading: Apply this to business reporting and analysis
steps.body: Synced slicers are especially useful when showcasing data like regional sales across several report pages, keeping the selected filter consistent throughout.

steps.heading: Wrap up your slicer setup
steps.body: Test your synced slicers across each page to confirm the filtering behaves as expected before sharing the report.

faq.question: Do synced slicers need to be visible on every page?
faq.answer: No, you can sync a slicer's filtering effect across pages without making the slicer itself visible on all of them.

faq.question: Can I sync only some pages instead of the whole report?
faq.answer: Yes, Power BI lets you choose exactly which pages a slicer syncs to, so you're not required to apply it across the entire report.

faq.question: Will syncing slicers slow down my report?
faq.answer: No, syncing slicers is a filtering configuration and doesn't add meaningful performance overhead to your report.

faq.question: Is slicer syncing available in all Power BI license tiers?
faq.answer: Slicer syncing is a core Power BI Desktop feature and is generally available regardless of your license tier.
